attributes.stem:
A 28-year-old woman presents to the antenatal clinic for routine follow-up at 28 weeks gestation with a dichorionic diamniotic twin pregnancy. She has no significant medical history and takes only pregnancy supplements. Her pregnancy has been uncomplicated until now.

On examination, she appears well with vital signs within normal limits. Serial ultrasound scans reveal:

Twin 1:
- Estimated fetal weight 1200g (50th centile)
- Normal amniotic fluid volume
- Normal umbilical artery Doppler

Twin 2:
- Estimated fetal weight 850g (below 5th centile)
- Normal amniotic fluid volume
- Normal umbilical artery Doppler
attributes.stemQuestion: Which one of the following is the most likely diagnosis?
options: Early twin reversed arterial perfusion sequence
attributes.explanation:
This case presents a dichorionic diamniotic twin pregnancy with significant growth discordance between the twins. The estimated fetal weights show a discordance of approximately 29% ((1200-850)/1200 x 100), which is significant.

Selective fetal growth restriction is the correct diagnosis, defined as one twin falling below the 10th centile for gestational age with a significant inter-twin growth discordance (usually >25%). This condition affects both monochorionic and dichorionic twins, though the management and prognosis differ between the two.

Twin-to-twin transfusion syndrome only occurs in monochorionic pregnancies where twins share a placenta with vascular anastomoses. It typically presents with oligohydramnios in one twin and polyhydramnios in the other, which is not present here.

Constitutional growth difference typically shows less marked discordance between twins (usually <20%) and both twins would still track along their respective growth centiles. The smaller twin in this case is below the 5th centile, indicating pathological growth restriction rather than constitutional difference.

Twin anaemia polycythaemia sequence is a rare condition affecting monochorionic twins, characterised by significant haemoglobin differences between twins due to chronic blood transfer through tiny placental anastomoses. It would not explain the growth discordance seen here.

Twin reversed arterial perfusion sequence is a rare complication of monochorionic twins where one twin lacks a heart (acardiac twin) and receives blood supply from the other twin. This condition is not compatible with the findings described in the stem where both twins have normal Doppler studies.
learningPoints: Growth discordance of >25% between twins suggests selective fetal growth restriction in multiple pregnancy.
Serial growth scans are essential for monitoring twin pregnancies, particularly to detect selective growth restriction.
Dichorionic diamniotic twins each have their own placenta and amniotic sac, but can still develop selective growth restriction.
references: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence (NICE). Twin and triplet pregnancy [Internet]. London: NICE; 2019 [cited 2024 Dec 4]. Available from: https://www.nice.org.uk/guidance/ng137/resources/twin-and-triplet-pregnancy-pdf-66141724389829.

D'antonio F, Prasad S, Masciullo L, Eltaweel N, Khalil A. Selective fetal growth restriction in dichorionic diamniotic twin pregnancy: systematic review and meta-analysis of pregnancy and perinatal outcomes. Ultrasound Obstet Gynecol. 2024 Feb;63(2):164-172. doi: 10.1002/uog.26302. Epub 2024 Jan 11. PMID: 37519089.
